2015-02-01.title: Public Survey Highlights Britons’ Global Ambitions

2015-02-02.abstract: Analysts who study terrorist groups said the militants’ tactics had backfired badly, particularly in Jordan.

2015-02-02.abstract: Dangerous times call for unusual measures in northwestern Pakistan, where the Taliban massacred 150 people at a school in December.

2015-02-03.abstract: A debate over what it means to be French — and whether that definition can make room for its growing Muslim population.

2015-02-03.title: Belgium: Man Accused of Threat Close to Parliament
2015-02-03.abstract: A man with a gun and a chain saw in his car was detained Monday by the police near the European Parliament, prompting the temporary evacuation of several buildings.

2015-02-03.abstract: Angela Merkel, on a visit with Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary, also said that sanctions against Russia for its actions in eastern Ukraine should remain in place.

2015-02-04.title: U.S. Official Says Cuba’s Political Detentions Declined

2015-02-04.abstract: Zacarias Moussaoui, a former Qaeda operative, said that members of the royal family were donors in the 1990s, and that he discussed plans to shoot down Air Force One with a staff member at the Saudi Embassy.

2015-02-04.abstract: The United Arab Emirates ended airstrikes in December after a Jordanian pilot was captured.

2015-02-05.abstract: American officials have praised the Saudi government for cracking down on terrorism, but suspicions of a past, tacit alliance with Al Qaeda burst into the open again this week.

2015-02-06.abstract: A weekly increase in new cases, the first this year, was worrisome because some of them were patients who had not been listed for monitoring, health officials said.

2015-02-07.abstract: The Security Council resolution, which could be adopted next week, is intended to choke the terrorist group’s ability to trade in oil, antiquities and hostages.

2015-02-25.abstract: A group of politicians and journalists is trying to buy a Hungarian restaurant long favored by London’s political left despite the acknowledged shortcoming of its menu.

2015-02-25.title: Ex-Member of Kazakhstan’s Inner Circle Dies in Vienna Jail
2015-02-25.abstract: Officials at a Vienna prison say the death of Rakhat Aliyev, former son-in-law of President Nursultan Nazarbayev of Kazakhstan, was clearly suicide, but Mr. Aliyev’s lawyers are not convinced.

2015-02-25.title: Police Kill Boy During Protest in Venezuela
2015-02-25.abstract: The interior minister, Carmen Meléndez, said that the officer had been arrested and that he had admitted to firing on the boy, Kluiberth Roa, with a shotgun loaded with plastic pellets.

2015-02-25.title: Monitors of Ukraine Conflict Seek Help
2015-02-25.abstract: Those in charge of the United Nations-mandated monitoring say more drones, radar systems and access to key areas are needed.

2015-02-26.title: Kerry Reminds Congress Netanyahu Advised U.S. to Invade Iraq
2015-02-26.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry recalled that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel strongly supported the United States’ invasion of Iraq.

2015-02-26.title: Shale Gas Project Encounters Determined Foes Deep in Algerian Sahara
2015-02-26.abstract: In Algeria, a country dependent on its oil and gas reserves, daily protests against a pilot shale gas project have turned into a national antifracking movement.

2015-02-26.title: Jordan: U.S. Warns of Threat to Malls
2015-02-26.abstract: The United States Embassy in Jordan on Wednesday warned American citizens to avoid high-end shopping malls in Amman, the capital, because of credible threats of terrorist attacks.

2015-02-26.title: South Korean Island Grows Wary After Welcoming the Chinese
2015-02-26.abstract: While many residents of the South Korean island of Jeju welcome the recent influx of Chinese investors, some worry about exploitation and becoming a pawn in Beijing’s power games.

2015-02-26.title: Talk Toughens as U.S.-Israel Relations Fray
2015-02-26.abstract: What started out as a dispute over the Israeli prime minister’s planned speech to Congress has consumed the two sides, threatening long-term consequences.

2015-02-27.title: 101-Year-Old Veteran Who Survived Home Invasion Dies in Canada
2015-02-27.abstract: Canadian media reported that the veteran, Ernest Côté, who was tied up and robbed in his home in December, had died of natural causes.

2015-02-27.title: Canada: Survivor of Home Invasion Dies
2015-02-27.abstract: The 101-year-old war veteran who was tied up and robbed in his Ottawa home in December, then left to suffocate with a plastic bag over his head, only to free himself and call the police, has died of natural causes.

2015-02-27.title: Rebels Accuse Saudis of Fueling Unrest to Divide Yemen
2015-02-27.abstract: The leader of the Houthi rebel group accused Saudi Arabia, Yemen’s powerful neighbor, of financing armed opponents and trying to divide the country.

2015-02-27.title: U.N. Official Seeks Havens for Syrians
2015-02-27.abstract: The United Nations high commissioner for refugees said that European countries must accept more Syrian refugees to dissuade them from making a perilous journey across the Mediterranean Sea.

2015-02-27.title: Historians Pore Over ISIS Video of Smashed Statues for Clues to What’s Been Lost
2015-02-27.abstract: Video released by the Islamic State shows jihadists taking sledgehammers and electric drills to ancient statuary in the city of Mosul.

2015-02-28.title: White House Offers Rebuttal Before Netanyahu’s Speech
2015-02-28.abstract: The Obama administration said Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu failed to present a feasible alternative to American proposals for curbing Iran’s nuclear program.

2015-02-28.title: Lawmakers (Just Not Greece’s) Approve a Bailout Extension
2015-02-28.abstract: Approvals of the bailout extension underscored how the political structure of Europe remains a work in progress.

2015-02-28.title: John Legend Rejects Calls to Cancel Bahrain Show Over Rights Abuses
2015-02-28.abstract: An American known for his outspoken advocacy of civil rights at home plans to perform in Bahrain next week, despite its brutal crackdown on dissent.

2015-02-28.title: Destruction of Antiquities by ISIS Militants Is Denounced
2015-02-28.abstract: The sculptures, statues and reliefs that were smashed by ISIS were crucial in the formation of early Arab identity, experts said.

2015-02-28.title: Diamond Thief Gets 15 Years in Paris Heist
2015-02-28.abstract: A French court concluded that Daoudi Yahiaoui, a convicted drug trafficker, was the brain behind a $100 million diamond heist at a Harry Winston jewelry store in 2008.
